stories.title: Ben Shelton Wins Stuttgart — First Grass Title, Three-Surface Season
stories.angle: Ben Shelton defeats defending champion Taylor Fritz 6-4, 2-6, 6-4 to claim his first career grass-court title and sixth overall. This is Shelton's third title of 2026 (Dallas hard, Munich clay, Stuttgart grass) — making him the first American to win titles on all three surfaces in a single season since Sam Querrey in 2010. He also saved three match points on his way to the title (one in R2, two in the SF). Shelton climbs to No. 5 in the ATP rankings, 320 points behind No. 4 Felix Auger-Aliassime.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Shelton d. Fritz 6-4, 2-6, 6-4. First grass title — his 3rd of 2026 on hard, clay AND grass. First American with titles on all three surfaces in one season since Sam Querrey in 2010. 🔥 #TennisFanrecap #BossOpen #Shelton

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: BEN SHELTON saved three match points on his way to the Stuttgart title — one in R2, two in the semifinal. Then beat defending champ Fritz for the crown. That's a complete week. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #BossOpen #Stuttgart

stories.title: Donna Vekic Wins Queen's Club as Lucky Loser — Bags First WTA 500 Title
stories.angle: Donna Vekic defeats Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6(6) to win the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club — arriving as a lucky loser after failing to qualify. Vekic entered only when Marta Kostyuk withdrew before the main draw. She becomes the first Croatian women's singles champion at Queen's Club, claims her first WTA 500 title, and rises from No. 76 to No. 32 in the rankings. Raducanu, who played double duty Saturday without dropping a set, saved two championship points in the second-set tiebreak but ultimately fell short of her first title since the 2021 US Open.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Donna Vekic entered Queen's Club as a lucky loser — lost in qualifying, got in when Kostyuk withdrew. Won the whole tournament. Beat Raducanu 6-0, 7-6(6) in the final. Improbable doesn't cover it. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #QueensClub #Vekic

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Raducanu played double duty Saturday, reached the final without dropping a set, and still lost to Vekic 6-0, 7-6(6). Still waiting for title No. 2 since the 2021 US Open. 🇬🇧 #TennisFanrecap #QueensClub #Raducanu

article.headline: From Lucky Loser to Champion: Vekic Stuns Raducanu to Win Queen's Club

research.facts:
Donna Vekic Wins Queen's Club as Lucky Loser
**Sources:** wtatennis.com, lta.org.uk, olympics.com, skysports.com, tennismajors.com, manilatimes.net
**Confirmed Facts:**
- [HIGH] Donna Vekic defeated Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6(6) to win the HSBC Championships (WTA 500) at Queen's Club, June 14, 2026 (Source: wtatennis.com, lta.org.uk, olympics.com)
- [HIGH] Vekic entered as a lucky loser after failing to qualify — she entered when Marta Kostyuk withdrew before the main draw (Source: tennismajors.com, wtatennis.com)
- [HIGH] This is Vekic's fifth career WTA title (Source: wtatennis.com, olympics.com)
- [HIGH] This is Vekic's first WTA 500 title (Source: wtatennis.com)
- [MEDIUM] This is Vekic's first title since 2023 (Source: tennismajors.com — "first in over three years, stretching back to 2023"; WTA official says "first since 2024" — discrepancy; using "first WTA 500 title" to avoid the discrepancy)
- [HIGH] Vekic rises from No. 76 to No. 32 in WTA rankings (Source: olympics.com, skysports.com)
- [HIGH] Vekic is the first Croatian women's singles champion at Queen's Club (Source: lta.org.uk, manilatimes.net)
- [HIGH] Raducanu entered the final having played double duty Saturday (QF + SF) without dropping a set all week (Source: from prior pipeline research, wtatennis.com)
- [HIGH] Raducanu saved two championship points in the second-set tiebreak (Source: lta.org.uk — "saving two second set points on the way" refers to Vekic saving; clarification: Raducanu forced the tiebreak to 6-6, Vekic won 8-6; LTA says "saving two second set points" = Raducanu saved 2 Vekic championship points before Vekic won)
- [HIGH] Raducanu has not won a title since the 2021 US Open (Source: consistent across multiple sources)
- [HIGH] Wimbledon begins June 29, 2026 (Source: lta.org.uk)

stories.title: Kamil Majchrzak Stuns de Minaur for First ATP Title at 's-Hertogenbosch
stories.angle: Kamil Majchrzak (Poland, No. 76) defeats Alex de Minaur 6-3, 2-6, 7-6(5) to win the Libema Open — his first ATP Tour title at age 30. Majchrzak beat three top-10 players in sequence: No. 4 Felix Auger-Aliassime (QF), No. 8 Daniil Medvedev (SF), and No. 6 de Minaur (final). His career-high ranking moves to No. 47 — first time in the Top 50. The achievement is especially remarkable given that Majchrzak served a 13-month suspension in 2023-24 for contaminated supplements. He becomes the third Polish man to win an ATP title in the Open Era after Wojtek Fibak and Hubert Hurkacz.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Majchrzak beat No. 4 Auger-Aliassime, No. 8 Medvedev, and No. 6 de Minaur at 's-Hertogenbosch — all in one week. Final: 6-3, 2-6, 7-6(5). First ATP title at 30. Career-high No. 47. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #LibemaOpen #Majchrzak

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Majchrzak served a 13-month suspension in 2023-24 for contaminated supplements. Came back. Ranked No. 76 a week ago. Now the 's-Hertogenbosch champion. One of tennis's great comeback stories. 🏆 #TennisFanrecap #LibemaOpen #Majchrzak

stories.title: ATP Rankings Update — Shelton No. 5, Majchrzak Debuts in Top 50
stories.angle: Post-grass week 1 rankings: Ben Shelton moves to No. 5 (320 points behind No. 4 FAA), Kamil Majchrzak enters the Top 50 for the first time at career-high No. 47 (+29). Shelton now has three 2026 titles — tied with Sinner for most on tour. Sinner remains No. 1 (13,500+ pts), Alcaraz No. 2. With Wimbledon draw set for June 26, the rankings battle for seedings is heating up.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Rankings after grass week 1: Shelton climbs to No. 5 (320 pts off No. 4 FAA). Majchrzak debuts in Top 50 at career-high No. 47. Sinner still No. 1. Wimbledon draw in 11 days. 📊 #TennisFanrecap #ATPRankings #Wimbledon2026

stories.title: Wimbledon 2026 — 14 Days Out, Form Guide Set
stories.angle: With all three grass-court week 1 tournaments now complete, the Wimbledon picture comes into focus. Championships start June 29, draw June 26, qualifying June 22-25. Jannik Sinner (defending champion, No. 1) has skipped all ATP grass warm-ups, attending only the Giorgio Armani Tennis Classic at Hurlingham (June 23-27). Carlos Alcaraz (No. 2) confirmed out with wrist injury. New this year: Video Review technology introduced at Wimbledon for the first time. Prize money: £64.2 million (record, 20% increase). Sizzling grass form: Shelton (Stuttgart W), Majchrzak (Libema W), Vekic (Queen's WTA W).

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: 14 days to Wimbledon. Draw June 26. Sinner defends (skipping ATP warm-ups). Alcaraz out. Hot form: Shelton (Stuttgart W), Majchrzak (Libema W), Vekic (Queen's WTA W). 🌿 #TennisFanrecap #Wimbledon2026 #GrassSeason
